children.heading: Task force and strategic plan
children.label: a Task force and strategic plan

children.heading: Task force
children.label: A Task force
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall establish a task force to develop and implement a strategic plan for enhancing the Secretary’s response to preventing and mitigating drug shortages.July 9, 2012

children.label: i
children.text: plans for enhanced interagency and intra-agency coordination, communication, and decisionmaking;

children.label: ii
children.text: plans for ensuring that drug shortages are considered when the Secretary initiates a regulatory action that could precipitate a drug shortage or exacerbate an existing drug shortage;

children.label: iii
children.text: plans for effective communication with outside stakeholders, including who the Secretary should alert about potential or actual drug shortages, how the communication should occur, and what types of information should be shared;

children.label: iv
children.text: plans for considering the impact of drug shortages on research and clinical trials; and

children.label: v
children.text: an examination of whether to establish a “qualified manufacturing partner program”, as described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: I
children.text: a “qualified manufacturer”, for purposes of such program, would need to have the capability and capacity to supply products determined or anticipated to be in shortage; and

children.label: II
children.text: in examining the capability and capacity to supply products in shortage, the “qualified manufacturer” could have a site that manufactures a drug listed under or have the capacity to produce drugs in response to a shortage within a rapid timeframe; andsection 356e of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: shall examine whether incentives are necessary to encourage the participation of “qualified manufacturers” in such a program.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: D Consultation
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the task force shall ensure consultation with the appropriate offices within the Food and Drug Administration, including the Office of the Commissioner, the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, the Office of Regulatory Affairs, and employees within the Department of Health and Human Services with expertise regarding drug shortages. The Secretary shall engage external stakeholders and experts as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: publish the strategic plan described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: submit such plan to Congress.

children.heading: Communication
children.label: b Communication
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that, prior to any enforcement action or issuance of a warning letter that the Secretary determines could reasonably be anticipated to lead to a meaningful disruption in the supply in the United States of a drug described under , there is communication with the appropriate office of the Food and Drug Administration with expertise regarding drug shortages regarding whether the action or letter could cause, or exacerbate, a shortage of the drug.section 356c(a) of this title

children.heading: Action
children.label: c Action
children.text: If the Secretary determines, after the communication described in subsection (b), that an enforcement action or a warning letter could reasonably cause or exacerbate a shortage of a drug described under , then the Secretary shall evaluate the risks associated with the impact of such shortage upon patients and those risks associated with the violation involved before taking such action or issuing such letter, unless there is imminent risk of serious adverse health consequences or death to humans.section 356c(a) of this title

children.heading: Reporting by other entities
children.label: d Reporting by other entities
children.text: The Secretary shall identify or establish a mechanism by which health care providers and other third-party organizations may report to the Secretary evidence of a drug shortage.
be subject to judicial review; or
be construed to establish a defense to an enforcement action by the Secretary.

children.heading: Temporary sunset
children.label: f Temporary sunset
children.text: Subsection (a) shall cease to be effective on the date that is 5 years after . Subsections (b), (c), and (e) shall not be in effect during the period beginning 5 years after , and ending on . Subsections (b), (c), and (e) shall be in effect beginning on .July 9, 2012July 9, 2012December 29, 2022December 29, 2022

any feedback or corrective or preventive actions in response to such reports.
